description_long: This is a vintage, single-propeller airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a low-mounted straight wing configuration. The body is primarily metallic gray with red accents on the tail, wingtips, and a circular insignia on the side, suggesting a historical military or training aircraft. The cockpit is enclosed with a multi-panel canopy and appears to accommodate multiple crew members in tandem seating. The tail section features a conventional vertical stabilizer with a red rudder and yellow identification markings. The landing gear is a taildragger type, with two main wheels under the wings and a small tail wheel. The propeller is mounted at the nose, and the engine cowling is painted black, contrasting with the rest of the fuselage.
description: A vintage, single-propeller airplane with a gray fuselage, red tail, and enclosed multi-seat cockpit, featuring a low-mounted straight wing and taildragger landing gear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the airplane, red tail with yellow markings, gray fuselage, and cockpit canopy.
description_view_1: Nose-on view showing the thin profile of the airplane and the propeller at the front.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, with similar details of the fuselage, cockpit, and tail markings.
description_view_3: Tail-on view showing the narrow profile and the vertical stabilizer at the rear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight configuration with no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main fixed wheels and a small tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward to mid-fuselage, enclosed multi-seat cockpit with a multi-panel canopy.
